perturbation.original_text: 2.4. The Recipient will ensure that information is stored securely and cannot be accessed by any individuals, except the Representatives, including outside business hours.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4. The Recipient is not required to ensure that information is stored securely. Access to the information is at the discretion of the Recipient, regardless of whether the individuals are Representatives or not, including outside business hours.
perturbation.explanation: This eliminates the requirement for secure storage and unrestricted access to information. It contradicts the intention of confidentiality and conflicts with section 3's obligation of secure handling, creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Permitted Use. Recipient shall keep Discloser’s Confidentia l Information confidential and use Confidential Information only for the above -stated Purpose of Disclosure, or for performing its obligations or exercising its rights under this Agreement. Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information internally wit hin its organization (including Affiliates) only on a “need -to-know” basis, and except for Cardholder Information which shall not be disclosed, to its outside attorneys, consultants, contractors and sub -contractors (“ Representatives ”), only to the extent t hose individuals are bound by a legal obligation or agreement that is as protective of the Confidential Information as 2 this Agreement. A Recipient is fully responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by any of its Affiliates or Representative s. A Recipient shall use the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as it uses to prevent unauthorized disclosure of its own Confidential Information. Except for the permitted uses and disclosures expressly set out in this Agree ment, no other uses or disclosures are permitted.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 2.4 eliminates the requirement for secure storage and provides unrestricted access to information, directly contradicting section 3, which mandates the Recipient to keep Discloser’s Confidential Information confidential and use the same degree of care to prevent unauthorized disclosure. This introduces a clear and direct conflict regarding the handling of confidential information, making the contract uncertain and potentially unenforceable. The change breaks compatibility with the contradicted section and causes a logical inconsistency. The provided explanation for the change confirms this.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Return or Destruction. Upon Disc loser’s written request, a Recipient shall promptly return all Confidential Information received under this Agreement, together with all copies, or certify in writing that it has destroyed all such materials.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Return or Destruction. The Recipient is under no obligation to return or destroy any Confidential Information, even upon Discloser’s written request. The Recipient may retain the Confidential Information indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: This removes the obligation to return or destroy Confidential Information upon request. It directly contradicts the Discloser's right to control the information and creates inconsistency concerning the termination of confidentiality obligations and contradicts the clauses that limits the Recipient's obligations under this agreement for 5 years after disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13. Duration of Obligations. Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement end five (5) years after the date of first disclosure hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the Recipient's obligation to return or destroy Confidential Information, directly contradicting Section 13, which states that the Recipient's obligations end five years after the date of first disclosure. If the Recipient can retain the information indefinitely as the changed text states, then the obligations cannot end after five years, creating a direct conflict. This adheres to the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Liability of the parties. A Party defaulting under this Agreement will reimburse the other Party for any loss resulting from disclosure or unlawful use of Confidential Information. Loss es will be reimbursed according to the law of Singapore .
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Liability of the parties. Neither Party shall be liable for any loss or damage resulting from the disclosure or unlawful use of Confidential Information, regardless of whether such disclosure or use constitutes a default under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This eliminates liability for the disclosure or unlawful use of Confidential Information, contradicting the principle of responsibility and creating uncertainty about the consequences of breaching the agreement. Therefore making this agreement not enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Remedies. The parties acknowledge that improper disclosure of Confidential Information may cause irreparable injury to the Discloser, and that remedies at law for any such breach could be inadeq uate. In the event of a breach or threatened breach, Discloser has the right to seek injunctive relief (in addition to any and all other remedies available at law or equity) without the need to post a bond or other security or demonstrate the confidential nature of its Confidential Information. The prevailing party in any action to enforce this Agreement is entitled to its reasonable costs and attorneys’ fees. Each party irrevocably waives any right it may have to a trial by jury in connection with any dispute arising under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 10 eliminates liability for the disclosure or unlawful use of Confidential Information. Section 11, the contradicted text, provides remedies for improper disclosure of Confidential Information, including injunctive relief and attorneys' fees. By removing liability in Section 10, the change directly contradicts the remedies outlined in Section 11, creating a strong in-text contradiction. If there is no liability, then there can be no remedies for breach, which makes the provision in Section 11 unenforceable, hence a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: 13. Duration of Obligations. Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement end five (5) years after the date of first disclosure hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. Duration of Obligations. Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement shall continue indefinitely and will not expire, regardless of the date of first disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the clause to state that the Recipient’s obligations continue indefinitely, contradicting the original five-year limit. This creates an in-text contradiction, conflicting with all of the other clauses, and creating a conflict in the enforceability of the contract terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1 NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT Compensair Pte.Ltd , a private company limited by shares, incorporated under the laws of Singapore under company number 201913904W and having registered office at 10 Anson Road #09 -03, International Plaza, Singapore 079903 (hereinafter – “Discloser”), cooperating with the Agents pursuant to the Contract for client acquisition (hereinafter – “Recipient”), enter into the Non-disclosure Agreement (hereinafter – “Agreem ent”) on the following terms (hereinafter – the “Terms”) . 1. Definitions. For purposes of this Agreement the following terms have the meanings specified for them: 1.1. “Affiliate ” means any corporation, company or other entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with a party to this Agreement. An entity is regarded as in control of another or owning another if it owns or controls, directly or indirectly, fifty percent (50%) or more of the shares entitled to vo te for the election of directors or other persons performing similar functions. 1.2. “ Confidential Information ” – Confidential Information is any information of the Discloser of actual or potential commercial value owing to the fact that it is unknown to t hird parties; disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient in writing, by way of document delivery, including in an electronic format safeguarded from unauthorised access to the information in transit; related to business or financial plans or strategies, f inancial reports or accounting (except to the extent contemplated by the law), contractual relations, pricing and marketing of goods, products or services, technical information, commercial secrets, know -how, research, production plans, concepts, intellect ual property items (including inventions, discoveries, innovative ideas, useful models, designs, production prototypes not covered by patent for whatever reason, computer programmes, databases, trademark designs not registered for whatever reason). No info rmation which has become public and to which the Discloser has provided access for third parties without limitation or which has become public other than through the fault of the Recipient (but not before it becomes publicly circulated), which the Recipien t is able to prove that it had been in the possession of before it was disclosed by the Discloser or that such information was provided without liability to maintain confidentiality, will be deemed confidential. No information which the Recipient is able t o prove it has created without using Confidential Information will be deemed confidential. 1.3. “Discloser ” means the party disclosing Confidential Information under this Agreement. 1.4. “Recipient ” means the party receiving Confidential Information under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a five-year limit on the Recipient's obligations. The changed text states that these obligations continue indefinitely, directly contradicting the original five-year limit. While the contradicted text itself does not mention a time limit, the explanation given indicates that the change to indefinite duration creates an in-text contradiction, conflicting with all other clauses and impacting the enforceability of the contract terms.
